question: A steel strip is welded to a copper strip of the same dimensions along the length. The coefficient of thermal expansion for copper is greater than that for steel. If temperature is increased, ______.
explanation: When the temperature is increased, the copper strip will expand more than the steel strip, causing the copper to be in tension and the steel to be in compression.
subject: Thermal Stress
ai_correct: When heated, copper tries to expand more than steel. Because they are bonded, steel restricts copper's expansion (putting copper in compression) and copper pulls steel (putting steel in tension).
ai_wrong: Students often assume both materials experience the same type of stress, failing to realize the bond forces them to reach a common equilibrium length.
trick: The material with the higher α will always be in compression when heated.
